description: Insect protein bar as novel food: authorisation, safety assessment, labelling, traceability

pipeline_inputs.query_text: insect protein novel food authorisation labelling requirements

ground_truth_requirements.description: Only novel foods authorised and included in the Union list may be placed on the EU market
Novel food must not pose a safety risk to human health, must not mislead consumers, and must not be nutritionally disadvantageous compared to the food it replaces
ground_truth_requirements.notes: May be extracted as authorisation rather than safety_assessment

ground_truth_requirements.description: Application for novel food authorisation must include name, description, production process, composition, scientific safety evidence, and proposed labelling conditions
Novel food must comply with specific conditions of use and labelling requirements set in the Union list entry
Post-market monitoring requirements may be imposed where specified in the authorisation decision
Food business operators must establish traceability at all stages and be able to identify suppliers and recipients of food products
Food shall not be placed on the market if it is unsafe (injurious to health or unfit for human consumption)
Mandatory food information: name, ingredients list, allergens, net quantity, date marking, storage conditions, nutrition declaration
